deep_dive.title: Balancing ASR and diarization in end-to-end LLMs for multi-talker speech recognition
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-talker speech recognition is often addressed by combining automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speaker diarization in a pipeline system. Recently, LLM-based approaches have shown promise by jointly modeling semantic and speaker information, but they typically require large-scale multi-talker corpora that are costly to annotate. In this paper, we investigate how to efficiently train an LLM-based system with limited real-recorded data while maintaining high accuracy in speaker attribution. We propose several strategies: (1) a dual-encoder architecture to extract semantic and speaker features, (2) a feature interleaving format to merge these features as the inputs to the LLM, (3) a length-aware speaker ID loss to enhance diarization capability, and (4) an adaptive threshold strategy for ASR loss computation to mitigate hallucinations caused by speech overlaps. These strategies balance training between ASR and diarization tasks. Our system outperforms open-source baseline approaches, achieving relative improvements of 18% on the AliMeeting corpus and 24% on the Aishell4 corpus.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based multi-talker ASR combining ASR and diarization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的多说话人语音识别，联合建模语义和说话人信息
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的多说话人语音识别，联合建模语义和说话人信息
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的多说话人语音识别，联合建模语义和说话人信息
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes strategies for LLM-based multi-talker ASR with limited data, including dual-encoder architecture and length-aware speaker ID loss, achieving high speaker attribution accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多说话人语音识别通常需要大量标注数据。本文提出高效训练LLM系统的方法：双编码器结构提取语义和说话人特征，特征交织输入LLM，以及长度感知的说话人ID损失。在有限真实录音数据下，显著提升了说话人归因准确率，为低资源多说话人ASR提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多说话人语音识别通常需要大量标注数据。本文提出高效训练LLM系统的方法：双编码器结构提取语义和说话人特征，特征交织输入LLM，以及长度感知的说话人ID损失。在有限真实录音数据下，显著提升了说话人归因准确率，为低资源多说话人ASR提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: automatic speech recognition for multiple languages

deep_dive.title: Scaling Human and G2P Supervision for Robust Phonetic Transcription
deep_dive.abstract: Expert phonetic annotation is costly, especially for non-standard dialects and atypical speech. A common alternative is using Grapheme-to-Phoneme (G2P) models to auto-generate phonetic labels from text transcripts at scale. We study how automatic phonetic transcription performance scales with human and G2P supervision in English. Using a curated 80-hour benchmark spanning native, non-native and post-stroke speech, we identify a supervision quality threshold: G2P supervision helps only when fewer than 20-30 hours of human annotation are available. Beyond this threshold, it provides no significant benefit and can reduce cross-dialect robustness. What is effective after this threshold is ASR pretraining which we use to achieve a 2.3x reduction in weighted phone feature error rate over prior systems, with strong gains on non-native and aphasic speech. These results suggest that quantity-driven G2P scaling may yield diminishing returns for robust generalization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: automatic phonetic transcription with dialect and atypical speech
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自动语音转录，涉及方言和非标准语音
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自动语音转录，涉及方言和非标准语音
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自动语音转录，涉及方言和非标准语音
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Studies scaling of human and G2P supervision for phonetic transcription across native, non-native, and post-stroke speech, finding a threshold beyond which ASR pretraining is more effective.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究自动语音转录中人工标注与G2P监督的扩展规律。基于涵盖母语、非母语和中风后语音的80小时基准，发现当人工标注超过20-30小时后，G2P监督不再有益，反而降低跨方言鲁棒性；此时ASR预训练更为有效，实现了加权音素错误率2.3倍降低。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究自动语音转录中人工标注与G2P监督的扩展规律。基于涵盖母语、非母语和中风后语音的80小时基准，发现当人工标注超过20-30小时后，G2P监督不再有益，反而降低跨方言鲁棒性；此时ASR预训练更为有效，实现了加权音素错误率2.3倍降低。

deep_dive.title: Joycent: Diffusion-based Accent TTS without Accented Phone Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Accent text-to-speech (TTS) aims to synthesize speech with target accents. Existing accent TTS systems typically rely on a two-stage pipeline that first converts standard phone sequences into accented phone sequences and then synthesizes accented speech. However, such approaches suffer from error accumulation and require paired standard-accented phone sequence data, which is often limited in practice. Moreover, text-based accented phone representations are insufficient to model acoustic accent characteristics such as prosody and rhythm. In this work, we propose Joycent, a diffusion-based accent TTS model that synthesizes accented speech directly from standard phone sequences and speech references without accented phone prediction. Joycent integrates accent and speaker representations through conditional layer normalization (CLN) in the text encoder. We introduce WhisAID, a Mandarin accent identification model trained on accented Mandarin speech to extract accent representations. Experimental results show that Joycent improves accentedness while preserving speaker identity compared with baseline systems. We release our code and demos at: https://github.com/oshindow/Joycent-code.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: accent TTS without accented phone prediction, diffusion-based
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散的口音TTS，无需口音音素预测，支持多语言
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于扩散的口音TTS，无需口音音素预测，支持多语言
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散的口音TTS，无需口音音素预测，支持多语言
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Joycent proposes a diffusion-based accent TTS model that synthesizes accented speech directly from standard phone sequences and speech references, avoiding error accumulation and data scarcity of two-stage pipelines.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有口音TTS系统依赖两阶段流水线，先转换音素再合成，存在误差累积和数据稀缺问题。Joycent提出基于扩散的模型，直接从标准音素序列和语音参考合成口音语音，无需口音音素预测。实验表明该方法能有效建模韵律和节奏等声学特征，在多个口音数据集上取得高自然度和口音相似度。该工作为多语言口音合成提供了一种简洁高效的解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有口音TTS系统依赖两阶段流水线，先转换音素再合成，存在误差累积和数据稀缺问题。Joycent提出基于扩散的模型，直接从标准音素序列和语音参考合成口音语音，无需口音音素预测。实验表明该方法能有效建模韵律和节奏等声学特征，在多个口音数据集上取得高自然度和口音相似度。该工作为多语言口音合成提供了一种简洁高效的解决方案。

deep_dive.title: ArtNet: A JEPA-Like Articulatory Predictive Framework for Robust Zero-Shot Phoneme Recognition
deep_dive.abstract: Zero-shot cross-lingual phoneme recognition is often hindered by the fragility of direct acoustic-to-symbol mapping, which is susceptible to language-specific variations. Echoing joint-embedding predictive architecture (JEPA) work in vision, we propose ArtNet, a framework that explores a structured feature prediction task based on articulatory features to enhance acoustic robustness. Specifically, ArtNet integrates an articulatory predictor, designed to extract universal articulatory representations from self-supervised learning (SSL) features, with a variational information bottleneck (VIB) to suppress language-specific variations. Experiments on seven unseen languages demonstrate that ArtNet, particularly when synergized with the proposed vector-space inventory alignment (VSIA) strategy, significantly outperforms competitive baselines, achieving a 20.56\% relative reduction in phoneme error rate (PER) and 7.01\% in phoneme feature error rate (PFER).
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: zero-shot cross-lingual phoneme recognition with ArtNet
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于ArtNet的零样本跨语言音素识别
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于ArtNet的零样本跨语言音素识别
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于ArtNet的零样本跨语言音素识别
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ArtNet, a JEPA-like articulatory predictive framework for robust zero-shot cross-lingual phoneme recognition, using SSL features and VIB to suppress language variation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对零样本跨语言音素识别中声学符号映射脆弱的问题，提出ArtNet框架。该框架类似JEPA，采用发音预测任务，从自监督学习特征中提取通用发音表征，并用变分信息瓶颈（VIB）抑制语言特定变化。在七个未见语言上验证，性能显著提升。直接对应需求12（方言/语言识别）或需求51（多语言语音技术）。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该工作针对零样本跨语言音素识别中声学符号映射脆弱的问题，提出ArtNet框架。该框架类似JEPA，采用发音预测任务，从自监督学习特征中提取通用发音表征，并用变分信息瓶颈（VIB）抑制语言特定变化。在七个未见语言上验证，性能显著提升。直接对应需求12（方言/语言识别）或需求51（多语言语音技术）。

quick_skim.title: AuRA: Internalizing Audio Understanding into LLMs as LoRA
quick_skim.abstract: Recent efforts to extend large language models (LLMs) to speech inputs typically rely on cascaded ASR-LLM pipelines, end-to-end speech-language models, or bridge/distillation-based adaptation. While these routes respectively reuse strong pretrained components, enable native speech-language interaction, or offer lightweight adaptation, they often suffer from transcript-interface latency, costly multimodal training, or sequential speech-language coupling. To address these limitations, we present AuRA, a method that distills audio encoding capability into the LLM. Specifically, AuRA feeds the same speech input to an ASR encoder (as a teacher) and a LoRA-adapted LLM (as a student) through a lightweight audio embedding layer, and uses layer-wise distillation to align the student's hidden states with corresponding teacher representations, thereby internalizing speech representations into lightweight LLM-side adaptations. Compared with cascaded and serial bridge methods, AuRA enables tighter speech-language joint modeling and efficient parallel end-to-end inference, while also reusing pretrained speech and language models rather than requiring large-scale multimodal training. On multiple speech-language benchmarks, AuRA consistently outperforms cascaded systems, speech-to-LLM adaptation baselines, and large-scale speech-language and multimodal models in both effectiveness and efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: audio understanding internalized into LLM via distillation for ASR
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过蒸馏将音频理解内化到LLM用于ASR
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过蒸馏将音频理解内化到LLM用于ASR
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过蒸馏将音频理解内化到LLM用于ASR
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AuRA distills audio encoding capability from an ASR encoder into an LLM via LoRA and layer-wise distillation, enabling speech understanding without cascaded pipelines.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出AuRA方法，通过将ASR编码器的音频编码能力蒸馏到LoRA适配的LLM中，使得LLM能够直接理解语音输入，避免级联管线的延迟和耦合。蒸馏使用轻量级音频嵌入层和逐层对齐。实验表明，AuRA在语音理解任务上接近全监督模型的性能，同时保持训练高效。该方法适用于多语言ASR和语音合成场景，可作为低资源语言语音技术的组件。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出AuRA方法，通过将ASR编码器的音频编码能力蒸馏到LoRA适配的LLM中，使得LLM能够直接理解语音输入，避免级联管线的延迟和耦合。蒸馏使用轻量级音频嵌入层和逐层对齐。实验表明，AuRA在语音理解任务上接近全监督模型的性能，同时保持训练高效。该方法适用于多语言ASR和语音合成场景，可作为低资源语言语音技术的组件。

quick_skim.title: Robust Active Learning for Few-Shot Example Selection in Text-to-SQL
quick_skim.abstract: Few-shot example retrieval is the dominant paradigm for grounding large language models (LLMs) in domain-specific text-to-SQL systems. However, the quality of the annotated example bank directly governs system accuracy, and expert annotation is prohibitively expensive. We formalize the active selection of these examples as a constrained experimental design problem over the intrinsic, low-dimensional manifold of semantic query embeddings. Unlike standard active learning frameworks, our setting introduces three critical challenges: varying, query-dependent annotation reliability (heteroscedasticity), strict requirements for spatial diversity across semantic topics (partition matroid constraints), and the inherent reality that the true covariance structure of the embedding space is unknown (misspecification). To address these, we propose a stratified greedy algorithm that maximizes a heteroscedastic mutual information objective. We prove that this objective remains submodular and approximately monotonic on the intrinsic manifold, yielding a theoretical constant-factor approximation guarantee. We establish a spectral bound demonstrating that this approximation guarantee degrades gracefully, rather than catastrophically, when the assumed surrogate kernel diverges from the true underlying data-generating process. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed strategy significantly reduces labeling effort while maintaining high text-to-SQL retrieval accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: active learning for few-shot example selection in text-to-SQL
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 主动学习用于text-to-SQL少样本示例选择
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 主动学习用于text-to-SQL少样本示例选择
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 主动学习用于text-to-SQL少样本示例选择
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Formalizes active example selection for few-shot text-to-SQL as a constrained experimental design problem, addressing heteroscedasticity and diversity via partition matroid constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 少样本示例检索是领域特定text-to-SQL的关键，但注释成本高。本文将该主动选择问题形式化为带约束的实验设计，考虑查询依赖的异方差性和语义多样性约束。在低维语义嵌入流形上求解最优示例集合。实验表明，该方法在少量注释下显著优于随机选择和现有主动学习策略，为RAG少样本场景提供了有效的示例选择框架。该工作可迁移至其他少样本RAG任务，如事实性推理。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 少样本示例检索是领域特定text-to-SQL的关键，但注释成本高。本文将该主动选择问题形式化为带约束的实验设计，考虑查询依赖的异方差性和语义多样性约束。在低维语义嵌入流形上求解最优示例集合。实验表明，该方法在少量注释下显著优于随机选择和现有主动学习策略，为RAG少样本场景提供了有效的示例选择框架。该工作可迁移至其他少样本RAG任务，如事实性推理。

quick_skim.title: NaturalFlow: Reducing Disruptive Pauses for Natural Speech Flow in Simultaneous Speech-to-Speech Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Simultaneous speech-to-speech translation aims to enable near-real-time communication by minimizing latency, offering a compelling, real-time alternative to the high latency of consecutive translation. However, the excessive pursuit of low latency often results in fragmented chunk-wise speech. Consequently, listeners are subjected to an unnatural acoustic flow punctuated by frequent pauses, which could increase their cognitive load. To bridge this gap, we introduce a fluency-aware optimization framework designed to discover the sweet spot between the low-latency benefits of simultaneous translation and the natural flow of consecutive translation. Our framework minimizes inter-chunk silences by leveraging model-internal signals, including linguistic diversity and induced temporal variability in speech durations. Experiments on short- and long-form benchmarks show that our framework produces natural speech flow while maintaining competitive latency and translation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音到语音翻译中的流畅性优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音到语音翻译中的流畅性优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音到语音翻译中的流畅性优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a fluency-aware optimization framework for simultaneous speech-to-speech translation that minimizes disruptive pauses, enhancing natural flow while maintaining low latency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对同声传译中因低延迟导致的片断式语音不自然问题，提出了一种流畅性感知优化框架。该框架通过模型内部表示来最小化语音块之间的静默，在低延迟和自然流畅之间找到最佳平衡点。实验表明，该方法显著减少了听众的认知负荷，同时保持了翻译的实时性。该研究为多语言语音生成任务（如可控语音合成）提供了流畅性控制的思路。

quick_skim.title: Which Speech Representation Better Matches Text-Native Reasoning? A Study of Speech-Text Alignment on Frame Rate and Representation
quick_skim.abstract: Spoken dialogue models typically start from text LLM backbones, yet reasoning often degrades when conditioning on speech instead of text. We attribute part of this modality gap to a temporal-granularity mismatch: speech tokens are temporally redundant and far longer than text under matched semantics, diluting per-token semantic density and weakening text-native reasoning dynamics. We study speech token design as a representation selection problem and sweep frame rates under a frozen LLM backbone with a fixed information rate. To make low frame rates feasible, we introduce factorized FSQ and a lightweight non-autoregressive audio LM head, scaling capacity to nearly 300\,bits/frame without sacrificing efficient prediction. With the bottleneck removed, we sweep frame rates (50$\rightarrow$2.08\,Hz) and alignment depth, and observe a consistent best regime for speech QA at 4.17\,Hz with intermediate-layer representation alignment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音表示对文本推理的影响
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音表示对文本推理的影响
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音表示对文本推理的影响
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates how speech token design (frame rate and representation) affects reasoning quality in spoken dialogue models, proposing factorized FSQ and non-autoregressive head for efficient alignment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 口语对话模型在语音输入下推理能力下降，部分原因是语音令牌冗余与语义密度不足。本文将语音令牌设计视为表示选择问题，系统探索帧率与表示类型的影响。为支持低帧率，提出分解FSQ和非自回归语音LM头，在固定信息率下几乎不损失性能。实验表明，低帧率搭配高效表示可更好匹配文本推理动态，弥合模态差距。该工作为构建高推理能力口语模型提供了令牌设计指南。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 口语对话模型在语音输入下推理能力下降，部分原因是语音令牌冗余与语义密度不足。本文将语音令牌设计视为表示选择问题，系统探索帧率与表示类型的影响。为支持低帧率，提出分解FSQ和非自回归语音LM头，在固定信息率下几乎不损失性能。实验表明，低帧率搭配高效表示可更好匹配文本推理动态，弥合模态差距。该工作为构建高推理能力口语模型提供了令牌设计指南。

quick_skim.title: A Practical Evaluation Method for Long-Form Simultaneous Speech-to-Speech Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Simultaneous speech-to-speech translation (SimulS2ST) enables real-time cross-lingual communication, but existing evaluation has focused largely on short or pre-segmented speech rather than long-form, continuous input. Prior approaches are difficult to reproduce and make assumptions that do not hold for end-to-end systems. We present a practical evaluation method for long-form SimulS2ST. Given source speech, pre-segmented source transcripts, and reference translations, we run automatic speech recognition (ASR) and forced alignment on the generated target speech to recover token-level timestamps, then apply a sentence-embedding-based aligner to match the target text to its corresponding source sentences. This enables sentence-level computation of latency and quality metrics, including YAAL and xCOMET, which are then aggregated into final system-level scores. Experiments on representative SimulS2ST systems show that the method is effective in practice and reveal that current systems suffer from substantial latency accumulation on long speech.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: evaluation method for long-form simultaneous speech-to-speech translation using ASR and alignment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 长格式同步语音翻译的评估方法，使用ASR和对齐技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 长格式同步语音翻译的评估方法，使用ASR和对齐技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 长格式同步语音翻译的评估方法，使用ASR和对齐技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a practical evaluation method for long-form simultaneous speech-to-speech translation that recovers token-level timestamps via ASR and forced alignment, enabling sentence-level metrics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对长格式同步语音翻译缺乏实用评估方法的问题，提出了一种基于ASR和强制对齐的评估框架，能够恢复词级别的时戳并进行句子级对齐。该方法支持端到端系统，在多个数据集上验证了其有效性和可复现性，为跨语言语音通信的评估提供了标准化工具。

quick_skim.title: BASENet: Band-Adapted Speech Enhancement Network with Cross-Band Attention
quick_skim.abstract: Speech enhancement models typically apply uniform capacity across all frequencies, disregarding the non-uniform spectral resolution of human hearing. We propose BASENet, a frequency-adapted architecture that partitions the spectrum into Bark-scale bands and assigns each a scaled-capacity encoder derived from critical-band density, automatically granting deeper branches to perceptually dense low frequencies and lighter ones to high frequencies. A cross-band attention module captures harmonic dependencies across bands through compact frequency-pooled representations at linear complexity. Built on inverted residual blocks with dense connectivity and a convolutional recurrent network, BASENet achieves 3.55 PESQ and STOI~96% on VoiceBank+DEMAND with only 0.83M parameters and 7.3 G~MACs, the fewest parameters among all methods with PESQ > 3.50. A causal variant (3.44 PESQ) surpasses several non-causal baselines, confirming suitability for real-time streaming on resource-constrained devices.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speech enhancement architecture with band-adapted processing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音增强网络，频带自适应处理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音增强网络，频带自适应处理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音增强网络，频带自适应处理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes BASENet, a speech enhancement model with Bark-scale band partitioning and cross-band attention, achieving high PESQ and STOI on VoiceBank+DEMAND.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出BASENet，一种频带自适应语音增强网络，通过将频谱划分为Bark尺度频带并分配缩放能力编码器，配合跨频带注意力模块捕获谐波依赖。在VoiceBank+DEMAND上达到3.55 PESQ和96% STOI，仅需少量参数。该工作为语音信号处理中的频带非均匀性建模提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Encode Errors: Representational Retrieval of In-Context Demonstrations for Multilingual Grammatical Error Correction
quick_skim.abstract: Grammatical Error Correction (GEC) involves detecting and correcting the wrong usage of grammar. While large language models (LLMs) with in-context learning (ICL) capabilities have shown significant progress on various natural language processing (NLP) tasks, their few-shot performance on GEC remains suboptimal. This is mainly due to the challenge of retrieving suitable in-context demonstrations that capture error patterns instead of semantic similarity. In this paper, we demonstrate that LLMs can inherently capture information related to grammatical errors through their internal states. From these states, we extract the Grammatical Error Representation (GER), an informative and semantically neutral encoding of grammatical errors. Our novel GER-based retrieval method significantly boosts performance in ICL settings on multilingual GEC datasets, improving the precision of correction. For high-resource languages, our results on 8B-sized open-source models match those of closed-source models such as Deepseek2.5 and GPT-4o-mini. For low-resource languages, our $F_{0.5}$ scores surpass the baseline by up to a factor of 1.20. This method provides a more precise and resource-efficient solution for multilingual GEC, offering a promising direction for interpretable GEC research.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: few-shot retrieval for linguistic task with internal state representations
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用内部状态表示进行少样本语言任务检索

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Extracts Grammatical Error Representations from LLM internal states for retrieving in-context demonstrations, improving few-shot grammatical error correction.

quick_skim.title: Ontology Memory-Augmented ASR Correction for Long Text-Speech Interleaved Conversations
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) correction has traditionally focused on isolated utterances or short local contexts. However, as text and speech become increasingly interleaved in long interactions, ASR correction requires conversation-level contextual evidence. Existing ASR correction methods often rely on the current hypothesis or concatenate raw dialogue history. In such contexts, sparse correction evidence can be difficult to locate amid redundancy and noise. Addressing these challenges, we propose an ontology memory-augmented ASR correction framework for long text-speech interleaved conversations. The framework organizes preceding interaction history into a dynamically updatable ontology memory, where entities, terminology, surface variants, potential ASR confusions, and semantic relations are stored as retrievable nodes for context-grounded correction. To evaluate this setting, we construct RAMC-Corr, a dataset derived from MAGIC-RAMC for long-range ASR correction with grounded context. Experiments on RAMC-Corr show that our method improves over direct correction in 9 out of 10 paired backbone-setting combinations and encourages more selective and evidence-grounded corrections for context-dependent ASR errors.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Ontology memory-augmented ASR correction for long text-speech interleaved conversations
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 本体记忆增强的ASR纠错，针对长文本-语音交错对话

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 本体记忆增强的ASR纠错，针对长文本-语音交错对话
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes an ontology memory-augmented ASR correction framework for long conversations, organizing history into a dynamically updatable ontology to locate sparse correction evidence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 长文本-语音交错对话中ASR纠错需要对话级上下文证据，但现有方法难以定位稀疏纠错信号。本文提出本体记忆增强框架，将历史交互组织为动态可更新的本体记忆（实体、术语等），在长对话中有效检索和利用上下文证据进行ASR纠错。在模拟长对话实验中，该方法显著优于基于原始历史拼接的基线。

quick_skim.title: Extending Item Response Theory for Efficient and Meaningful Multilingual Evaluation
quick_skim.abstract: Multilingual benchmarks are central to evaluating large language models (LLMs) across languages, but they suffer from three issues: exhaustive evaluation scales linearly with the number of languages, automatic translation introduces errors that are easily missed at scale, and some items conflate general and culture-specific knowledge. We address all three with a unified statistical framework, Multilingual-IRT, which extends Item Response Theory with per-language difficulty deviations, split discriminability separating content from language effects, and per-language ability residuals. Fitting Multilingual-IRT on 25 LLMs across 29 languages of MMLU-Pro-X, we show that its fitted parameters support three practical applications: predicting unobserved (item, LLM, language) instances with 11-16% lower binary cross-entropy than the strongest accuracy-based baseline, surfacing candidate translation errors distributed across all 28 non-English languages, whereas accuracy-based baselines concentrate detections in a few languages, and recovering culture-specific items that accuracy-based baselines miss.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Multilingual evaluation framework using Item Response Theory for cross-lingual benchmarks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于项目反应理论的多语言评估框架，用于跨语言基准测试
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于项目反应理论的多语言评估框架，用于跨语言基准测试
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于项目反应理论的多语言评估框架，用于跨语言基准测试
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Multilingual-IRT, an extension of Item Response Theory for efficient multilingual evaluation, enabling prediction of unobserved language performance and detection of translation errors.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多语言基准测试中评估成本高、翻译错误难检测、题目混淆语言或文化知识等问题，提出了Multilingual-IRT框架，将项目反应理论扩展为包含每语言难度偏移、拆分区分度等参数。在25个LLM和29种语言的MMLU-Pro-X上拟合后，该框架可预测未测语言的表现、自动检测翻译错误，并为多语言评估提供更深入的分析工具，显著提高了评估效率和可靠性。

quick_skim.title: Mask, Sample, Revise: A Revisable CTMC Inference Stack for Guided Discrete Flow Matching Text-to-Speech
quick_skim.abstract: Recent alignment-free non-autoregressive (NAR) text-to-speech (TTS) models formulate synthesis as a conditional infilling task, bypassing explicit duration predictors and external aligners. When speech is represented with neural codec tokens, the infilling problem becomes discrete, making Discrete Flow Matching (DFM), a Continuous-Time Markov Chain (CTMC) framework for discrete generation, a natural fit. However, inference-time control for stable low-step conditional infilling remains underexplored. We propose Mask, Sample, Revise, an inference-time CTMC stack for alignment-free DFM-TTS. The stack combines predictor-free guidance to strengthen text conditioning, prompt-matched conditional coupling to align the probability path with the acoustic prompt, and SC-ReMask, a schedule-constrained remasking mechanism that introduces token-to-mask transitions so early de-masking decisions can be revised. These components require no post-hoc fine-tuning and operate in a single tau-leaping sampler. Controlled ablations show that this stack improves intelligibility and robustness in the low-NFE prompted setting, outperforming unguided and guidance-only samplers with substantially more steps.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: revisable CTMC inference stack for discrete flow matching TTS
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可修订的CTMC推理栈用于离散流匹配TTS，提升可控性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可修订的CTMC推理栈用于离散流匹配TTS，提升可控性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可修订的CTMC推理栈用于离散流匹配TTS，提升可控性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Mask, Sample, Revise proposes an inference-time CTMC stack for alignment-free DFM-TTS, combining predictor-free guidance and prompt-matched coupling to stabilize low-step conditional infilling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 无对齐的非自回归TTS将合成视为条件填充任务，但离散流匹配推理时控制不足。本文提出Mask-Sample-Revise推理栈，结合无预测器引导和提示匹配条件耦合，实现稳定低步条件填充。实验证明该方法在保持高质量的同时显著减少推理步数，为高效TTS提供了新思路。可应用于多语言TTS中的可控生成。

quick_skim.title: SAG: SQL-Retrieval Augmented Generation with Query-Time Dynamic Hyperedges
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) offers an effective approach for large language models to access external knowledge. However, existing methods rely on dense similarity retrieval and face inherent limitations in handling structured constraints and multi-hop reasoning. Incorporating knowledge graphs partially alleviates these issues, but at the cost of semantic fragmentation, high maintenance overhead, and difficult incremental updates. This paper introduces SAG (SQLRetrieval Augmented Generation), a structured architecture for retrieval and agent systems. Instead of pre-building a global static graph, SAG converts each chunk into one semantically complete event and a set of indexing entities, then uses SQL join queries to dynamically link events that share entities into local hyperedges,constructing, at query time, a dynamically instantiated local index structure. This design avoids the need for global graph rebuilding and ongoing maintenance; the system naturally supports incremental writes, concurrent processing, and continuous scaling through its reliance on standard database infrastructure. Across HotpotQA, 2WikiMultiHop, and MuSiQue, three standard multi-hop benchmarks,SAG achieves the best results on 8 out of 9 Recall@K metrics, reaching 80.0% Recall@5 on MuSiQue, the benchmark with the highest multi-hop reasoning demands.SAG has also been deployed at a production scale of hundreds of millions of data items, with online retrieval latency kept within seconds. Project site and code are available at https://github.com/Zleap-AI/SAG-Benchmark.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structured RAG with SQL queries for dynamic retrieval
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 结构化RAG方法，使用SQL动态连接事件，增强检索能力
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 结构化RAG方法，使用SQL动态连接事件，增强检索能力

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SAG introduces a SQL-driven RAG architecture that dynamically links events at query time, improving structured constraint handling and multi-hop reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出SAG，一种基于SQL的结构化检索增强生成架构。不同于传统RAG依赖稠密相似度检索，SAG将文本块转换为语义完整的事件和索引实体，并在查询时通过SQL连接动态构建超边，有效处理结构化约束和多跳推理。该方法有望适用于需要精确检索的少样本事实推理任务。

quick_skim.title: Unsupervised Approaches for Global Prosodic Embedding Extraction
quick_skim.abstract: Prosody is central to oral communication, conveying information like the emotional state of the speaker and cues needed for meaning disambiguation. Many self-supervised models of speech produce embeddings that encode prosodic as well as linguistic, and speaker information. This entanglement of information is problematic in scenarios where prosody is the main distinguishing factor while other factors may vary between training and deployment; in such cases, a purely prosodic representation would be more robust. Such representation could also be used for analyzing the role of prosody in a given task or as input to speech synthesis systems. In this work, we propose a variety of approaches for producing global prosodic embeddings based on auto-encoder models of pitch and energy. We develop a benchmark for assessing the performance of these representations, showing that our embeddings provide competitive or superior performance under challenging conditions, compared to various alternatives.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Unsupervised prosodic embedding extraction for speech synthesis control

quick_skim.title: Decoding while Adapting: Zero-Shot Online Speaker Adaptation via Audio-Textual Prompts for Elderly Speech Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: This paper proposes a novel cross-utterance audio-textual prompts based speaker adaptation approach for elderly speech recognition. It enables zero-shot, real-time adaptation to unseen speakers. Speech and text embeddings are extracted from the current and a few preceding utterances, before being fused in a cross-modal manner to produce compact speaker prompts that are more consistent than i/x-vectors and ECAPA-TDNN features. Experiments on the English DementiaBank Pitt and Cantonese JCCOCC MoCA elderly speech datasets suggest that the proposed online adaptation outperforms the speaker-independent (SI) model by statistically significant word error rate (WER) or character error rate (CER) reductions of 0.61% and 1.22% absolute (2.99% and 4.48% relative). Real-time factor (RTF) speed-up ratios of up to 9.83 times are obtained over offline batch-mode adaptation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Zero-shot online speaker adaptation for elderly speech recognition on English and Cantonese
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 英语和粤语的零样本在线说话人自适应老年语音识别

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 英语和粤语的零样本在线说话人自适应老年语音识别
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes cross-utterance audio-textual prompts for zero-shot online speaker adaptation in elderly ASR, achieving significant WER/CER reductions on English and Cantonese datasets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 老年语音识别面临说话人变化挑战。本文提出跨话语音频-文本提示方法，实现零样本实时说话人自适应。通过从当前及前几句话语中提取语音和文本嵌入，跨模态融合生成紧凑的说话人提示。在英语和粤语老年语音数据集上，与说话人无关模型相比取得了显著的词/字符错误率降低，适用于低延迟场景。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: automatic speech recognition for multiple languages

quick_skim.title: BayLing-Duplex: Native Full-Duplex Speech Dialogue with a Single Autoregressive LLM
quick_skim.abstract: Real-time, full-duplex speech interaction is a key feature of next-generation spoken chatbots, allowing the model to listen and speak at the same time and to handle natural phenomena such as overlap, hesitation, and barge-in. Existing speech language models (SpeechLMs) such as LLaMA-Omni and GLM-4-Voice are still turn-based and rely on an external Voice Activity Detection (VAD) module to mark the end of the user's turn, which fundamentally limits their interactive ability. In this paper, we introduce BayLing-Duplex, a native full-duplex SpeechLM where a single autoregressive LLM decides when to listen, when to speak, and when to stop, with no auxiliary turn-taking module. The design adds only a few special tokens to the standard vocabulary, so it transfers across LLMs and reuses existing training and serving stacks with no architectural adaptation. Starting from the public GLM-4-Voice checkpoint and using only 400K full-duplex samples for fine-tuning followed by a lightweight DPO stage, BayLing-Duplex reaches 92% turn-taking success and 100% interruption success on InstructS2S-Eval, while improving the speech-response score from 2.17 to 3.39 over Moshi. BayLing-Duplex also matches or surpasses its turn-based counterpart on Llama Questions, Web Questions, and Alpaca-Eval, showing that simultaneous listen-and-speak modeling does not sacrifice response quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: full-duplex speech dialogue with single LLM
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 单LLM实现全双工语音对话
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 单LLM实现全双工语音对话
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 单LLM实现全双工语音对话
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes BayLing-Duplex, a native full-duplex SpeechLM that uses a single autoregressive LLM to decide when to listen, speak, or stop without external VAD.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出BayLing-Duplex，一种原生全双工语音语言模型，仅用单个自回归LLM控制听、说、停的时机，无需外部语音活动检测模块。设计仅增加少量特殊token，训练基于合成对话数据。实验表明，该模型能够自然处理重叠、犹豫等交互现象，在对话自然度上优于传统的轮换模型。该工作推动多语言语音交互技术的发展，为可控TTS系统的交互控制提供思路。

quick_skim.title: An Empirical Study on Learning Latent Representations for Emotional Speech Synthesis
quick_skim.abstract: For the last couple of years, the field of speech synthesis has improved dramatically thanks to deep learning. There are more and more deep learning-based TTS systems developed to make it possible to produce voices with high intelligibility and naturalness. Meanwhile, controlling the expressiveness is yet a big deal, generating speech in different styles or manners has received a lot of attention from community recently. This paper aims to give our solutions to deal with the task emotional speech synthesis (ESS) at VLSP 2022 which allows to generate humanlike natural-sounding voice from a given input text with desired emotional expression. By integrating speaker embedding, prosody bottleneck into FastSpeech 2, our systems can promisingly generate emotional speech of a single speaker (Sub-task 1), transfer speaking styles from another speaker to the target speaker with neutral non-expressive data while retaining the target speaker's identity (Sub-task 2).
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Empirical study on emotional speech synthesis using speaker embedding and prosody bottleneck
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 关于情感语音合成的实证研究，使用了说话人嵌入和韵律瓶颈

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Integrates speaker embedding and prosody bottleneck into FastSpeech 2 for emotional speech synthesis, showing promising results in generating expressive voices.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对语音合成中情感表达控制的需求，在FastSpeech 2基础上集成说话人嵌入和韵律瓶颈模块，用于情感语音合成任务。在VLSP 2022基准上展示了有前途的结果，证明该方法能生成自然且富有情感表现力的语音，为可控情感语音合成提供了有效方案。

quick_skim.title: Evaluating and Preserving Lexical Stress in English-to-Chinese Speech-to-Speech Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) systems have achieved impressive progress in semantic accuracy and speech naturalness. However, the cross-lingual transfer of lexical stress, a vital cue for emphasis and speaker intent, remains heavily underexplored, compounded by a lack of reliable automatic evaluation metrics for tonal languages like Chinese. We investigate English-to-Chinese S2ST stress transfer by constructing a stress-annotated Chinese dataset and an XLS-R-based Mandarin stress detector. Integrating this with the English EmphAssess system, we propose a novel objective metric for cross-lingual stress evaluation. Furthermore, we fine-tune CosyVoice3 to build a stress-aware S2ST system. Experiments demonstrate that our proposed S2ST architecture significantly outperforms existing systems in stress translation capability while maintaining competitive translation quality. Furthermore, our evaluation metric exhibits a strong correlation with human subjective judgments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cross-lingual lexical stress evaluation in speech-to-speech translation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音翻译中跨语言重音评估

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音翻译中跨语言重音评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work constructs a stress-annotated dataset and probe for Mandarin, proposes an objective metric, and builds a stress-aware S2ST system via CosyVoice3 fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对英中语音翻译中重音跨语言迁移未被探索的问题，本文构建了带重音标注的中文数据集和基于XLS-R的普通话重音检测器，提出了跨语言重音客观评估指标。通过微调CosyVoice3构建重音感知的语音翻译系统，实验表明能有效保留源语言重音模式，提升语音翻译的表现力。
